title: carex muricata subsp. furculata
intro: Carex muricata subsp. furculata, also known as the rough-stalked sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The rough-stalked sedge has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting digestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
